reasoning: Despite the simulated scenario in which the U.S. has conducted extensive airstrikes against Iranian nuclear sites (Operation Midnight Hammer in June 2025) and deployed a Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU) to the Middle East in October 2025, the probability of an offensive intended to 'establish control' over Iranian territory before December 31, 2025 remains very low. Military experts universally note that a full ground invasion would require hundreds of thousands of troops, which the U.S. has not visibly amassed. Furthermore, the 2,200 troops in the deployed MEU are tactically suited only for rapid strike-and-withdraw raids, not holding or establishing definitive control over territory or islands, as sustaining a garrison on Iranian soil would leave U.S. troops highly vulnerable to mainland-based missile attacks. Without evidence of a massive long-term troop buildup necessary to seize and hold terrain, and with only 52 days remaining in the year, an offensive to establish control is extremely unlikely to occur.
